title: Run self-improvement workflow as atomic memory sync
decision: Execute self-improvement workflow runs as one cohesive chain that updates patterns, iteration history, project context, and decisions in the same session.
rationale: Partial updates create stale or inconsistent memory artifacts and reduce the reliability of follow-up automation.

alternatives_considered.option: Update only selected memory files ad-hoc
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Increases drift risk and weakens reproducibility of future runs.

title: Self-improvement workflow is composed from 5 base skills
decision: Treat self-improvement as an orchestrated workflow that chains session-bootstrap, pattern-extractor, iteration-logger, context-keeper, and skill-generator.
rationale: A standalone self-improvement skill was not installed, but all required primitives already exist and are stable.

alternatives_considered.option: Wait for a separate single skill package
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Would block immediate usage even though all building blocks are available now.

title: Backlog expanded with runtime hardening priorities
decision: Add priorities P19-P23 to address async route blocking, pending-hit lifecycle, calibration modularization, multi-cam fusion burst handling, and app-state concurrency contracts.
rationale: These risks were directly observed during current codebase analysis and coverage verification.

alternatives_considered.option: Continue only with existing backlog items
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Would leave currently observed runtime risks undocumented and unprioritized.

title: Async route waits centralized for lifecycle and calibration paths
decision: Use centralized asyncio-based wait helpers in src.web.routes for release delays and state polling instead of inline time.sleep calls inside async handlers.
rationale: Blocking waits in async routes can stall the FastAPI event loop during pipeline transitions and calibration capture, especially while MJPEG and WebSocket traffic are active.

alternatives_considered.option: Keep inline time.sleep loops and accept short stalls
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Short stalls still block the shared event loop and become user-visible under load.

title: Pending-hit lifecycle centralized server-side
decision: Manage pending hit candidate insertion, expiry, overflow trimming, lookup, and clearing through shared helpers in src.main, with lifecycle counters exposed via /api/stats.
rationale: A shared backend policy prevents stale candidates from surviving disconnected clients and keeps route/UI behavior aligned with the authoritative server state.

alternatives_considered.option: Keep timeout handling primarily in the frontend and only patch individual routes
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: It leaves the backend vulnerable to stale state and duplicates lifecycle semantics across layers.

title: Runtime-state lifecycle centralized around deterministic lifespan startup
decision: Use shared state helpers in src.utils.state for runtime-state initialization, pipeline references, thread handles, and multi-frame cache mutation, and treat FastAPI lifespan startup as the authoritative reset boundary.
rationale: Shared app_state lifecycle fields were otherwise mutated inconsistently across routes, worker threads, and tests, making concurrency behavior and startup assumptions harder to reason about.

alternatives_considered.option: Keep open dict writes and relax deterministic startup so existing tests continue to pass
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: It preserves hidden coupling and weakens the runtime contract instead of making tests match real startup behavior.

title: Calibration workflows split behind helper modules with stable manager API
decision: Keep CalibrationManager as the public facade, but move board workflow logic, shared config IO/defaults and repeated ChArUco observation collection into dedicated helper modules.
rationale: This reduces calibration-module complexity and improves direct testability of persistence and error paths without forcing route or manager API changes.

alternatives_considered.option: Rewrite calibration call sites to use new manager types directly
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: It would widen the change surface and risk API regressions in a calibration-critical area.

title: Multi-camera fusion now buffers short burst windows per camera
decision: Replace the one-slot-per-camera fusion buffer with a small per-camera time-window buffer and pair detections around the oldest pending anchor while removing only consumed entries.
rationale: Burst detections from the same camera should not overwrite each other before fusion, and timeout behavior should be reproducible and time-ordered instead of depending on whichever detection arrived last.

alternatives_considered.option: Keep the latest-only buffer and only tweak timeout thresholds
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: It leaves burst overwrites unsolved and does not make fusion order deterministic.

title: Historically tracked Python cache artifacts were removed from the Git index
decision: Keep the existing ignore rules, remove tracked __pycache__ and related cache paths from the Git index, and document the cleanup step in the development workflow.
rationale: Legacy tracked artifacts keep the worktree noisy across sessions and obscure real code changes, while .gitignore alone does not fix already tracked cache paths.

alternatives_considered.option: Leave the tracked cache files in the repository and rely only on .gitignore for future runs
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: The worktree would remain noisy and future sessions would continue to see false-positive changes.
